title: Psalms, Chapter 110
description: Psalms chapter 110. Both the manuscript page scan AND the Greek text are taken directly from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century AD) via the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). The Greek transcription preserves the actual uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra abbreviations, and lectional marks of the manuscript. Library folio: 119. English translation verbatim from the King James Version (1611, public domain) via bible-api.com.
introduction:
This entry meets the strict three-criteria standard of the Theosis Library, with the manuscript text drawn directly from the same source as the scan:

  1. Manuscript scan: The actual folio of Codex Sinaiticus containing this passage, from the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). Library folio reference: 119.
  2. Original text: The verbatim transcription of the Sinaiticus manuscript from the Codex Sinaiticus Project, preserving uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra (e.g. ⟨θϲ⟩ = θεός), and scribal marks. Nomina sacra are shown in angle brackets to indicate the manuscript's sacred-name abbreviations.
  3. English translation: The King James Version (1611), retrieved verbatim from the public-domain bible-api.com endpoint.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>αλληλουϊαεξομολογηϲομαι ϲοι κε εν ολη καρδια μου εν βουλη ευθιων και ϲυναγωγη <sup>2</sup>μεγαλα τα εργα κυ εξεζητημενα ειϲ παντα τα θεληματα αυτου <sup>3</sup>εξομολογηϲειϲ και μεγαλοπρεπια το εργον αυτου και η δικαιοϲυνη αυτου μενει ειϲ τον αιωνα του αιωνοϲ <sup>4</sup>μνειαν εποιηϲατο των θαυμαϲιων αυτου ελεημων και οικτιρμων ο κϲ <sup>5</sup>τροφην εδωκεν τοιϲ φοβουμενοιϲ αυτον μνηϲθηϲεται ειϲ τον αιωνα διαθηκηϲ αυτου
translation.text:
<sup>1</sup>The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou at my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool. <sup>2</sup>The LORD shall send the rod of thy strength out of Zion: rule thou in the midst of thine enemies. <sup>3</sup>Thy people
shall be willing in the day of thy power, in the beauties of holiness from the womb of the morning: thou hast the dew of thy youth. <sup>4</sup>The LORD hath sworn, and will not repent, Thou
art a priest for ever after the order of Melchizedek. <sup>5</sup>The Lord at thy right hand shall strike through kings in the day of his wrath.

translation.original_text: <sup>6</sup>ϊϲχυν εργων εαυτου ανηγγειλεν τω λαω εαυτου του δουναι αυτοιϲ κληρονομιαν εθνων <sup>7</sup>εργα χειρων αυτου αληθια και κριϲιϲ πιϲται παϲαι αι εντολαι αυτου <sup>8</sup> <sup>9</sup>λυτρωϲιν απεϲτιλεν τω λαω αυτου ενετιλατο ειϲ τον αιωνα διαθηκην αυτου αγιον και φοβερον το ονομα αυτου <sup>10</sup>αρχη ϲοφιαϲ φοβοϲ κυ ϲυνεϲιϲ αγαθη παϲι τοιϲ ποιουϲιν αυτην η αινεϲιϲ αυτου μενει ειϲ τον αιωνα του αιωνοϲ
translation.text:
<sup>6</sup>He shall judge among the heathen, he shall fill
the places
 with the dead bodies; he shall wound the heads over many countries. <sup>7</sup>He shall drink of the brook in the way: therefore shall he lift up the head.
